This is all very shady - we deserve a better government than this!

In an extraordinary parliamentary hearing on Tuesday, a mandarin sacked by the Prime Minister revealed the 'atmosphere of pressure' created by No 10 to approve the New Labour grandee as ambassador to the US, as well as its 'dismissive approach' to security vetting. And the scandal took another turn when Sir Olly Robbins revealed that Downing Street had secretly tried to give another plum diplomatic job to Sir Keir's spin doctor. In a stinging criticism of the PM's judgment, Energy Secretary Ed Miliband publicly admitted he had been against giving Mandelson the prestigious Washington DC posting, well before it was known he had failed his security clearance test. Asked what he had thought about the appointment at the time, Mr Miliband told Sky News: 'That it could blow up, that it could go wrong. I had a conversation with David Lammy about it before the appointment, and I said I was worried about it. I think he was worried about it too.'
  • 31 comments

an absolute disgrace, the man should be getting an award not the sack ,this is us well on the road to the end of civilised society

Beverley Callard became emotional as she watched back her I'm A Celebrity South Africa exit on Tuesday night, revealing she had no idea she was battling breast cancer. The series was pre-recorded in Kruger National Park last year, with the actress, 69, forced to leave on medical grounds after 'a funny turn'. Four months after filming, she was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer.  In an Instagram video, Beverley explained what the incident was that prompted her to leave on medical grounds and how it was the 'start' of her battle with cancer.

I thought I'd quit my addiction to 'tweakments' and Botox forever. Then, feeling particularly confident at a Dubai lunch, I asked a stranger to guess my age...

Picture the scene. A charity lunch at a glitzy restaurant in Dubai. I am talking to an elderly Asian tycoon, who, because he had been eating at a table by himself, I thought might appreciate the attention. A portrait of Rachel Reeves finalising her first Budget has been acquired for the Parliamentary Art Collection. The work by Sally Ward was unveiled with little fanfare at the end of last month after a competition run by the Society of Women Artists. Commons sources said the winning artist was awarded an 'acquisition prize' of £3,000, which came from the budget of the cross-party Speaker's Advisory Committee on Works of Art. But critics insisted spending public money on the painting showed a 'total lack of self-awareness' as Brits face the prospect of a record tax burden and the fallout from the Middle East crisis.

I remember how thrilled my mother had been when she got a 100th birthday greeting from the Queen. We framed it and had it on her dresser. For those 100 year olds that were capable of attending today, this would have meant a lot to them.

The King led a rousing rendition of 'Happy Birthday' for three men and women celebrating their centenaries on what would have been his own mother's 100th birthday today. Unusually he was accompanied at Buckingham Palace by a full turn-out of working royals, from youngest to oldest: the Prince and Princess of Wales, the Duke and Duchess of Edinburgh, the Princess Royal, the Duke and Duchess of Gloucester and even the Duke of Kent and Princess Alexandra - the last two aged 90 and 89 respectively. Kate shared an emotional moment with 88-year-old George Cross hero Tony Gledhill, whom she hugged. He later explained that his wife had recently died, but had told him beforehand that he must attend the palace event regardless.

  • 4 videos

I see the snow leopard

The stunning picture was taken by NASA's Hubble space telescope which is celebrating it's 36th birthday this week. Situated about 5,000 light-years from Earth, is a star-formation region called the Trifid Nebula which Hubble captured in all its cosmic wonder. The breathtaking image, captured using Hubble's Wide Field Camera 3, shows a shimmering cloud of gas and dust where new stars are forming. In visible light, the colours resemble an underwater landscape, with fine particles appearing to drift like sediment through the depths. But this latest observation highlight a distinctive formation within the nebula, described by astronomers as a 'Cosmic Sea Lemon' due to its resemblance to a sea slug gliding through space.
  • 5 comments
  • share Space scientists spot a 'sea slug'... can you see it?

A Florida man, Chadwick Scott Willacy, was executed for the brutal 1990 murder of his neighbor Marlys Sather, whom he beat, bound, and set on fire after she discovered him burglarizing her home. Sather, pictured right alongside her only son, John, died from smoke inhalation, indicating she was still alive when he set her ablaze, and Willacy was sentenced to death after multiple court proceedings and appeals.His execution marks Florida's fifth in 2026, as the state continues to lead the nation in executions under Governor Ron DeSantis.

  • Situationship? Who makes up these words? I pity anyone trying to learn English as a second language these days
  • Too much money. Not enough brain cells.
  • Married him after 3 months 😂I wonder what attracted her to this multi millionaire ! 😂and she up the duff , can u imagine if any of her go stars on MIC did what she has! She would slate them something terrible
  • They completely wasted £1300 on an Airbnb. They'd already managed 5 days in the hotel and only had 2 more to go. But the fact that they couldn't all lounge about together as a family in their pyjamas for a week was ludicrous. The money could have been put to so much better use.

Love to see a man in a waistcoat. There's something very cool about that look. And hats. Bring back hats. So smart.

A great-grandfather has splashed out thousands of pounds on making his life a real-life Peaky Blinders... on the fourth Sunday of every month.  Derek Brennan, 67, has spent at least £10,000 recreating the fictional 1920s gangster look. His four-bedroom home in Birmingham contains a staggering eight wardrobes full of paraphernalia. The former skinhead, who grew up on a council estate in Dublin before moving to England at the age of 16, has a vast collection of three-piece suits, hats of all varieties and pocket watches, synonymous with fictional Tommy Shelby's on-screen look.
  • 40 comments
  • share How a great-grandfather spent £10,000 on Peaky Blinders gear

I was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes in August 2023. I immediately embarked on a keto diet which I faithfully followed and within 3 months I had lost 25 lbs and my blood sugar was normal. In the subsequent 3 months I lost further 25 lbs. since then I limit my carb intake although not necessarily keto, my blood sugar remains normal and my weight has remained the same. I have considerably more energy and drive. I am 80 this year.

A high-fat, low-carbohydrate ketogenic diet could help improve how the pancreas functions in people with type 2 diabetes, according to a new study.
  • 40 comments
  • 1 video
  • 23 shares Type 2 diabetes patients 'could benefit from keto diet'

Email

If I was sight impaired or blind, I would want a real, furry, intelligent loyal friend as my eyes, no question

The visually impaired in China are trialling robot guide dogs.  The robo-pups can respond to their human's voice and communicate with them through a voice interaction system to allow them to move around safely. A trial run in Chengdu, in China's Sichuan Province, showed a woman holding the 'lead' as the four-legged robot led her down the street.
  • 4 comments
  • share Man's metal best friend? Visually impaired people test new guide dogs
